summary: DeVault pushes back against GNOME's claim that client-side decorations (CSD) are the settled standard for Wayland, arguing that no consensus exists. He outlines real problems with CSD including inconsistent look and feel, inability to control misbehaving clients, and lost compositor customization. He accuses GNOME of pushing 'disingenuous propaganda' and refusing compromise, noting he was banned from the #wayland IRC channel for advocating discussion. He announces that Sway, KDE, and other wlroots-based compositors have developed a protocol extension supporting server-side decorations as an alternative.
key_points: GNOME's claim that CSD is settled and SSD will never work on Wayland is false — no actual consensus was reached
CSD creates real problems: inconsistent UI, inability to manage misbehaving windows, and loss of compositor-level customization like tabbed windows
Sway and KDE collaborated on a Wayland protocol extension allowing compositors and clients to negotiate decoration responsibility
GNOME has refused compromise and silenced dissent, including banning DeVault from the #wayland IRC channel
money_quotes: Though GNOME may imply that a consensus has been reached, this is not the case.
We are willing to cooperate on a compromise, but GNOME does not want to entertain the discussion and would rather push disingenuous propaganda for their cause.
I have been banned from this channel for over a year because I persistently called for compromise.
GNOME's statement that '[server-side decorations] do not (and will never) work on Wayland' is false.
KDE and Sway have long agreed on the importance of these problems and have worked together on a solution.
key_insight: GNOME's unilateral declaration that client-side decorations are settled policy for Wayland is false, and Sway, KDE, and other compositors have built a working protocol extension proving server-side decorations are viable.
